description: Gracie's Atlaria worldbuilding notes — characters, species, kingdoms, organizations, lore, and story drafts, restructured from School Notes.docx.

articles.title: Atlantis "Atla" Sirian
articles.summary: Jiyra MMC, age 25. Charming, flirty, hides his true nature among humans. Last of his line after dragons killed his parents.

content.text: Reasons: “Atla” is short for Atlantis. He dislikes his dead name. Artemis calls him “Atlas” because she mistakenly called him that when they first met. Finnian and Teirick are his fake names while hiding.
Eyes: Dark, stormy gray — usually half-lidded in concentration
Hair: Blonde with dirty-blonde highlights, short, falls to the back of his neck
Distinguishing marks: Small scar on left side of lip
Good traits: Ambivert — typically more extroverted
Bad traits: Struggles to fit in, lonely
Good habits: Light tread from a lifetime of sneaking, practically soundless. Stays in human form. Discreet.
Mood: Charming, extroverted, flirty, occasionally annoying
Sense of humor: Makes a lot of jokes
Slight British accent. Pitch shifts often — gets high when scared or high-energy. Medium register at baseline.
Changes name to keep his identity hidden
Currently blending in as a human
Only eats meat — throws up if he has to eat anything plant-based
Prefers to work alone despite being extroverted
Likes: Exploring, horse-riding (despite being bad at it), venison, meat, action, drama
Not religious. A little rebellious against the human empire.
Most at ease: Around Artemis, around crowds
Most uneasy: With dragons or wyverns, when they are mentioned, around hostile people
Mental state: Carries trauma from his past
Dragons killed his parents, making him one of the last Jiyra. After being orphaned he learned to thieve and to defend himself.
He meets Artemis by asking if there is a place for him to stay. They meet again later when he finds her reading alone. They share names. They part. They keep finding each other.
Not very emotionally intelligent. Quick and concise. Works out a lot — physical strength outpaces emotional regulation.

articles.contentText:
Identity

Full name: Atlantis Sirian

Meaning: A sunken city

Nicknames: Atla, Atlas, Finnian, Teirick (Teir)

Reasons: “Atla” is short for Atlantis. He dislikes his dead name. Artemis calls him “Atlas” because she mistakenly called him that when they first met. Finnian and Teirick are his fake names while hiding.

Gender: Male

Pronouns: He/Him

Sexuality: Bisexual, Biromantic

Race: Jiyra

Age: 25 (appears 22)
Appearance

Height: 6 ft

Weight: 152 lb

Build: Lean, lithe, athletic

Face shape: Round

Eyes: Dark, stormy gray — usually half-lidded in concentration

Eyesight: 20/20

Hair: Blonde with dirty-blonde highlights, short, falls to the back of his neck

Skin: Pale, fair

Distinguishing marks: Small scar on left side of lip

Dominant hand: Left

Overall: Charming, pretty, soft
Personality

Good traits: Ambivert — typically more extroverted

Bad traits: Struggles to fit in, lonely

Good habits: Light tread from a lifetime of sneaking, practically soundless. Stays in human form. Discreet.

Bad habits: Flirts with everybody

Mood: Charming, extroverted, flirty, occasionally annoying

Sense of humor: Makes a lot of jokes
Voice
Slight British accent. Pitch shifts often — gets high when scared or high-energy. Medium register at baseline.
Quirks

Changes name to keep his identity hidden

Currently blending in as a human

Only eats meat — throws up if he has to eat anything plant-based

Prefers to work alone despite being extroverted
Skills

Skilled archer

Hunting

Charming people

Telling jokes

Disguising himself

Thieving

Sneaking

Kissing

Falconry
Likes & Dislikes

Likes: Exploring, horse-riding (despite being bad at it), venison, meat, action, drama

Dislikes: Dragons, wyverns

Fears: Being lonely
Beliefs
Not religious. A little rebellious against the human empire.
Triggers

Most at ease: Around Artemis, around crowds

Most uneasy: With dragons or wyverns, when they are mentioned, around hostile people

Mental state: Carries trauma from his past
Backstory
Dragons killed his parents, making him one of the last Jiyra. After being orphaned he learned to thieve and to defend himself.
He meets Artemis by asking if there is a place for him to stay. They meet again later when he finds her reading alone. They share names. They part. They keep finding each other.
Flaws
Not very emotionally intelligent. Quick and concise. Works out a lot — physical strength outpaces emotional regulation.
Theme
Bittersweet Symphony. “Perfect” by Ed Sheeran.

articles.title: Draconia
articles.summary: The apex family of Atlaria. The only known family in the order Draconiformes.

content.text: Draconia is the only known family of Draconiformes. They are distinguishable by their key characteristics: two wings, forked tongues, scales, the ability to speak Draconian, and carnivorous diets. They are the apex animals of Atlaria.

Draconia are thought to have evolved from the long-extinct Ancestral Dracian, which lived alongside the Proto-Animals. The Ancestral Dracian eventually evolved into the Proto-Dragons, which were key for the present-day Draconian species to evolve.
Modern Draconia are smaller, more common versions of their ancestors. Modern Draconia consists of Wyvernia, Dragon-kin, and Jiyra.
Ancient Draconia consists of Dragons, Drakes, Wyrms, and Sea Serpents — all believed extinct.

articles.contentText:
Overview
Draconia is the only known family of Draconiformes. They are distinguishable by their key characteristics: two wings, forked tongues, scales, the ability to speak Draconian, and carnivorous diets. They are the apex animals of Atlaria.
Origins
Draconia are thought to have evolved from the long-extinct Ancestral Dracian, which lived alongside the Proto-Animals. The Ancestral Dracian eventually evolved into the Proto-Dragons, which were key for the present-day Draconian species to evolve.
Taxonomy

Domain: Eukarya

Kingdom: Animalia

Phylum: Chordata

Class: Reptilia

Order: Draconiformes

Family: Draconic
Ancient vs. Modern
Modern Draconia are smaller, more common versions of their ancestors. Modern Draconia consists of Wyvernia, Dragon-kin, and Jiyra.
Ancient Draconia consists of Dragons, Drakes, Wyrms, and Sea Serpents — all believed extinct.
Living Major Branches

True Draconia: Wyvernia

False Draconia: Dragon-kin, Jiyra

articles.title: Wyverns
articles.summary: The only remaining species of true ancestry Draconia. Two wings, two legs.

content.text: Wyverns are the only remaining species of the ancestral Draconia/Draconic line. They all have two wings and two legs.
Wyverns serve as mounts for the Dragon-kin. The Maned Aero is the only domesticated subspecies. Emperor Aero are the apex of mountain ecosystems.

articles.contentText:
Overview
Wyverns are the only remaining species of the ancestral Draconia/Draconic line. They all have two wings and two legs.
Taxonomy

Family: Draconic

Genus: Wyvern

Species: Aero
Place in the World
Wyverns serve as mounts for the Dragon-kin. The Maned Aero is the only domesticated subspecies. Emperor Aero are the apex of mountain ecosystems.

articles.title: Aero
articles.summary: The wyvern species: smaller, more common Wyverns found in a variety of places.

content.text: Smaller, more common Wyverns that can be found in a variety of places. The Aero species is the foundation for several distinct subspecies.

articles.contentText:
Overview
Smaller, more common Wyverns that can be found in a variety of places. The Aero species is the foundation for several distinct subspecies.
Sub-species

Maned Aero

Emperor Aero

Whiskered Aero

Saber-toothed Aero

articles.title: Maned Aero
articles.summary: The only domesticated Wyvern subspecies — the mount of choice for Dragon-kin.

content.text: Maned Aero are the only kind of domesticated Wyvern used as mounts for Dragon-kin. They have thick, furry manes with a feline-like face, large, long horns protruding from the top of their heads, fluffy tails, and feathered wings. Their backs are flat and adapted for saddles. Their eyes are very cat-like.
They come in colors of beige and mahogany. Males have bright-colored horns to attract females.
They are slightly intelligent — enough to convey feelings to their Dragon-kin bonded — though they do not form full, English-like thoughts.
Maned Aero typically live in hot areas such as savannahs, though they nest near cliffs.

articles.contentText:
Description
Maned Aero are the only kind of domesticated Wyvern used as mounts for Dragon-kin. They have thick, furry manes with a feline-like face, large, long horns protruding from the top of their heads, fluffy tails, and feathered wings. Their backs are flat and adapted for saddles. Their eyes are very cat-like.
Coloration
They come in colors of beige and mahogany. Males have bright-colored horns to attract females.
Intelligence
They are slightly intelligent — enough to convey feelings to their Dragon-kin bonded — though they do not form full, English-like thoughts.
Habitat
Maned Aero typically live in hot areas such as savannahs, though they nest near cliffs.
Anatomy

Short fur covers the body

Paws instead of talons

Feathered wings

Long curved horns

Cat-like face and eyes
Taxonomy

Family: Draconic

Genus: Wyvern

Species: Aero

Sub-species: Maned Aero

articles.title: Emperor Aero
articles.summary: The largest of the Aero — apex predators of mountain ecosystems.

content.text: Emperor Aero are the largest of the Aero — the apex of their ecosystems. They live in mountains and use their large, segmented wings to glide from cliff to cliff, though they can fly for long distances as well.
Karu, bonded to Kya, is a male Emperor Aero.

articles.contentText:

articles.title: Sun Breather
articles.summary: A massive Colossal — the only living Draconia species that can still vomit flame.

content.text: Sun Breathers are massive Colossals — the only living species of Draconia that can vomit flames.
They tower 36 feet at the shoulder. Their long, flexible necks let them reach up to 60 feet tall in a defensive posture.
Large antler-like horns that tower above the head
Sun Breathers vomit golden flames at high temperatures. The flame is so bright it can blind those with weak eyes.
The most intelligent of Draconia, though this is often a disadvantage — they are prideful and reckless by nature.

articles.contentText:
Description
Sun Breathers are massive Colossals — the only living species of Draconia that can vomit flames.
Size
They tower 36 feet at the shoulder. Their long, flexible necks let them reach up to 60 feet tall in a defensive posture.
Anatomy

Large antler-like horns that tower above the head

Curved horns under the antlers

Huge feathered wings (unusual for Draconics)
Fire Breath
Sun Breathers vomit golden flames at high temperatures. The flame is so bright it can blind those with weak eyes.
Intelligence & Temperament
The most intelligent of Draconia, though this is often a disadvantage — they are prideful and reckless by nature.
Coloration
Yellow, gold, orange.

articles.title: Aether Lion
articles.summary: A slightly smaller Colossal — celestial-looking, with star-speckled fur and feathers.

content.text: Aether Lions are a slightly smaller species of Colossal that stand 30 feet tall at the shoulder. Their flexible necks bring them to a total height of about 40 feet when raised.
Feathered wings, with one claw on each
Large lion-like mane along the neck
Long fur lining down the spine ending in a tail tuft
Massive antlers with additional spikes on the forehead
Blue, purple, and black, speckled with white on the feathers and fur — patterns that resemble stars.

articles.contentText:

Aether Lions are a slightly smaller species of Colossal that stand 30 feet tall at the shoulder. Their flexible necks bring them to a total height of about 40 feet when raised.
Anatomy

Long fur lining down the spine ending in a tail tuft

Blue, purple, and black, speckled with white on the feathers and fur — patterns that resemble stars.

articles.title: Dragons
articles.summary: An extinct genus of Draconia — widely worshipped, even in their absence.

content.text: Dragons are an extinct genus of the ancestry Draconia/Draconic, and are widely worshipped by most sapients including the Dragon-kin, Jiyra, and some Humans.
Even in extinction, dragons remain the touchstone of religious feeling for Dragon-kin and Jiyra. Their absence is felt as a wound.
When and why did they go extinct?
Rumors of any last surviving individual?

articles.contentText:
Overview
Dragons are an extinct genus of the ancestry Draconia/Draconic, and are widely worshipped by most sapients including the Dragon-kin, Jiyra, and some Humans.
Cultural Status
Even in extinction, dragons remain the touchstone of religious feeling for Dragon-kin and Jiyra. Their absence is felt as a wound.
To develop

When and why did they go extinct?

Any surviving fossils, scales, hoards?

Rumors of any last surviving individual?

articles.title: Dragon-kin
articles.summary: Human in form, descended from the extinct dragons, bonded with wyverns. Rivals to humans.

content.text: Dragon-kin appear as humans, though they are closely connected to dragons and wyverns, sharing a symbiotic bond with them.
The Dragon-kin are descended from the now-extinct dragon.
The only kind of creature that understands Wyvern tongue. They share close bonds with the most intelligent of the wyverns.
They share a long, old rivalry with the humans.

articles.contentText:
Overview
Dragon-kin appear as humans, though they are closely connected to dragons and wyverns, sharing a symbiotic bond with them.
Heritage
The Dragon-kin are descended from the now-extinct dragon.
Abilities
The only kind of creature that understands Wyvern tongue. They share close bonds with the most intelligent of the wyverns.
History
They share a long, old rivalry with the humans.
Society
Their kingdom is Jandora.

articles.title: Jiyra
articles.summary: Shapeshifters — humans who can turn into dragons. The closest living link to the extinct dragons.

content.text: Very few are left. Closely related to Dragon-kin. They appear as humans but can shapeshift into dragons — often called "false dragons." Of any living creature, they are the closest related to the extinct dragons.
Folklore tells that Jiyra are deadly demons straight from hell. They are hunted for being "cursed." Most Jiyra blend in with the humans.
They contain an inner warmth that stems from their ability to breathe fire. They live nearly as long as dragons, growing to be thousands of years old.
It is often very hard to master shapeshifting — usually spontaneous, correlating to the wielder's emotions.
Atlantis "Atla" Sirian is one of the last known Jiyra.

articles.contentText:
Overview
Very few are left. Closely related to Dragon-kin. They appear as humans but can shapeshift into dragons — often called "false dragons." Of any living creature, they are the closest related to the extinct dragons.
Folklore & Persecution
Folklore tells that Jiyra are deadly demons straight from hell. They are hunted for being "cursed." Most Jiyra blend in with the humans.
Biology
They contain an inner warmth that stems from their ability to breathe fire. They live nearly as long as dragons, growing to be thousands of years old.
Shapeshifting
It is often very hard to master shapeshifting — usually spontaneous, correlating to the wielder's emotions.
Notable
Atlantis "Atla" Sirian is one of the last known Jiyra.

articles.title: Humans
articles.summary: “Mankind.” Believe they are superior to other peoples; descendants of the elves.

content.text: “Mankind.” They believe they are superior to others. According to their own histories, they are descendants of the elves.
Their kingdom is Kaelor — described as a cruel society.
They share a long, old rivalry with the Dragon-kin, and persecute Jiyra as demons.

articles.contentText:

Their kingdom is Kaelor — described as a cruel society.
Tensions
They share a long, old rivalry with the Dragon-kin, and persecute Jiyra as demons.

articles.title: Elves
articles.summary: Extinct. Remnants suggest swifter, more agile humans with strange antler attachments.

content.text: Extinct. Remnants found of them seem to be swifter, more agile humans with strange antler attachments to the head.
Humans claim descent from the elves.
Are any remnants still living in hiding?

articles.title: Fanged Warthog
articles.summary: Carnivorous warthog with a toothy mouth. Has a taste for human flesh.

content.text: A warthog that has a toothy mouth. Carnivores that have a taste for human flesh.

A warthog that has a toothy mouth. Carnivores that have a taste for human flesh.

articles.title: Tree Lion
articles.summary: Mid-sized arboreal felines. Both sexes carry proto-manes.

content.text: Large felines with small "proto-manes" that resemble the manes of young lions. Both females and males have these manes. They live in the trees and have a similar lifestyle to a leopard, though they are slightly bigger — still smaller than lions.
Black and brown, resembling tabby cats.

articles.contentText:
Description
Large felines with small "proto-manes" that resemble the manes of young lions. Both females and males have these manes. They live in the trees and have a similar lifestyle to a leopard, though they are slightly bigger — still smaller than lions.
Coloration
Black and brown, resembling tabby cats.

content.text: Horses are typically used as mounts for humans. Dragon-kin ride wyverns; humans ride horses.

articles.title: Artemis — Daughter of the Hunt
articles.summary: The creator goddess of Atlaria. The world was born from her tears.

content.text: Daughter of the Hunt. Creator of Atlaria. Watcher over its creatures from the Cosmos.

Artemis was a majesty held in the eyes of mortals — once tricked by her own brother, Apollo, to shoot her only love, Orion, through the head. Sadness beheld her and she sank to the bottomless depths of a void.
Many millennia passed as she cried. Her tears soaked and filled the void with water, water that became life and life again. She looked from her tears and beheld her creation — her own world.
The world grew into life and life and life. Mortals were born — mortals were worshippers of her majesty.

Artemis, consumed by her love for the new world at hand, let a new species come forth: the Dragon. From the dragon sprouted life and life and life — the Wyvern, the Jiyra, and more. But the Jiyra came upon the Dragon, and events led after one another — the first Dragon-kin was birthed, new life!
Artemis returned to the Cosmos, where she continued her life as a goddess, still keeping a watchful eye upon her creation in the void.
The hunter's bow. The moon. A bow drawn beneath a full crescent.
Mortals across Atlaria — Dragon-kin most reverently, Jiyra by inheritance, some Humans by tradition.
Atla is "not religious." His own story is named after a sunken city — there is irony in carrying a name that belongs to her grief while denying her worship.

articles.contentText:
Domain
Daughter of the Hunt. Creator of Atlaria. Watcher over its creatures from the Cosmos.
Creation Myth
Artemis was a majesty held in the eyes of mortals — once tricked by her own brother, Apollo, to shoot her only love, Orion, through the head. Sadness beheld her and she sank to the bottomless depths of a void.
Many millennia passed as she cried. Her tears soaked and filled the void with water, water that became life and life again. She looked from her tears and beheld her creation — her own world.
The world grew into life and life and life. Mortals were born — mortals were worshippers of her majesty.
Artemis, consumed by her love for the new world at hand, let a new species come forth: the Dragon. From the dragon sprouted life and life and life — the Wyvern, the Jiyra, and more. But the Jiyra came upon the Dragon, and events led after one another — the first Dragon-kin was birthed, new life!
Artemis returned to the Cosmos, where she continued her life as a goddess, still keeping a watchful eye upon her creation in the void.
Symbol
The hunter's bow. The moon. A bow drawn beneath a full crescent.
Followers
Mortals across Atlaria — Dragon-kin most reverently, Jiyra by inheritance, some Humans by tradition.
Relationship to Atla
Atla is "not religious." His own story is named after a sunken city — there is irony in carrying a name that belongs to her grief while denying her worship.

articles.title: The Mongoose and the Cobra
articles.summary: A fable about courage — fearlessness vs calculation — told through two mortal enemies.

content.text: The Mongoose is fearless and social. The Cobra is calculated and shy. They are mortal enemies, and they teach each other a lesson.
Courage incorporates both fearlessness and calculation. Both can achieve the same goal through different ways of being brave.
Both fight over territory, survival, offspring
The story itself — what does Llyr do, what does Rakari do?
Cultural context — who tells this myth, around what fire?
Are Llyr and Rakari mortal creatures, or symbols?
Connection to the named dragon "Rakari the Black Death" — same Rakari, or shared name?

articles.contentText:
The Story
The Mongoose is fearless and social. The Cobra is calculated and shy. They are mortal enemies, and they teach each other a lesson.
Theme
Courage incorporates both fearlessness and calculation. Both can achieve the same goal through different ways of being brave.
What They Share

Both are scared of something

Both fight over territory, survival, offspring
Characters

The Mongoose: Llyr

The Cobra: Rakari
Adjacent Names

Kazini

Zaylari

Freayza

Yaela

Akzi

Bryn
Siblings (motif?)

The Flayer

The Fletcher
To develop

The story itself — what does Llyr do, what does Rakari do?

Cultural context — who tells this myth, around what fire?

Are Llyr and Rakari mortal creatures, or symbols?

Connection to the named dragon "Rakari the Black Death" — same Rakari, or shared name?

articles.title: Dragon Riders & The Cost of Riding
articles.summary: Worldbuilding notes on the real-world cost of dragon mounts — taming, anatomy, behavior, altitude.

meta.source: Bond — symbiotic relationship between rider and creature

rules.text: Dragons cannot be tamed by force alone — taming depends entirely on the dragon's intelligence and consent (animal vs sapient vs monstrous).
Maintaining a dragon mount has enormous material cost — meat, lair, equipment.
Dragon anatomy must support being ridden — back smoothness, wing & spine clearance, size.
Dragon behaviour must be compatible — territoriality, hibernation, fire-marking.
High-altitude flight requires breathing equipment, harness, and cold-weather gear for the rider.

limits.text: Sapient dragons raise rights questions — riding may not be ethically possible.

content.text: Dragon riders and dragon riding in general is hands down one of the favorite tropes in all of fantasy. There's something undeniably cool about befriending and riding on the back of such a large, powerful magical beast. Having dragon mounts is a massive boon for any army — flying over barricades and destroying whole legions, plus the psychological damage from sheer intimidation.
However, for as amazing as the idea is, the cost and drawbacks of dragon riders are rarely mentioned. These are the obstacles wanna-be dragon riders need to overcome.
Whether dragons are even tameable is the biggest hurdle. This depends entirely on the intelligence of the dragons themselves — whether they are monsters, animals, or sapient beings.
Monstrous dragons are violent and destructive — untameable to most without dark magic or a greater evil will.
Sapient dragons raise consent issues — you need a dragon who will agree to be used as a mount, with all the rights questions that brings.
Animalistic dragons may be best for taming, but apex predators are difficult to tame and remain dangerous no matter how well trained.

Dragons would be ridiculously expensive to keep. Large carnivorous animals are expensive in any pre-industrial society; meat was very costly. Tame dragons would need huge amounts of meat per individual, and even more for:
Restraints big and strong enough to contain them
A large lair / stable for off-duty time
Saddles, reins, and other equipment scaled to a dragon
A treasure hoard, if the species needs one
Horses are our main mounts because their anatomy is well-suited to being ridden. Dragons are not so neat.
A dragon's back may not be smooth
Wings, scales, and back-spines complicate saddle design
A dragon has to be a specific size — too small can't bear a rider, too large and a human can't mount or control them
A dragon needs to be tameable AND trainable AND have the right attitude.
Large, solitary apex predators tend to be territorial and defiant
They may not get along with other dragons, or with their supposed master
Hibernation or seasonal migration removes them from service for long periods
Fire-breathing used for territorial marking risks burning your home down in a rampage
Lack of oxygen and high flying speed. Rarely mentioned — flying at high altitudes means lack of oxygen and cold, strong winds. Riders need:
Heavy jackets and goggles for warmth and wind
Some kind of oxygen tank or enchanted mask to allow breathing
Safety harnesses and straps so a strong gust doesn't throw them off
Use the Cast Sandbox above to test specific "what if a Dragon-kin tried to do X with their bonded Aero" scenarios against these rules.

articles.contentText:
Why Write This Down
Dragon riders and dragon riding in general is hands down one of the favorite tropes in all of fantasy. There's something undeniably cool about befriending and riding on the back of such a large, powerful magical beast. Having dragon mounts is a massive boon for any army — flying over barricades and destroying whole legions, plus the psychological damage from sheer intimidation.
However, for as amazing as the idea is, the cost and drawbacks of dragon riders are rarely mentioned. These are the obstacles wanna-be dragon riders need to overcome.
Obstacle 1: Tameability
Whether dragons are even tameable is the biggest hurdle. This depends entirely on the intelligence of the dragons themselves — whether they are monsters, animals, or sapient beings.

Monstrous dragons are violent and destructive — untameable to most without dark magic or a greater evil will.

Sapient dragons raise consent issues — you need a dragon who will agree to be used as a mount, with all the rights questions that brings.

Animalistic dragons may be best for taming, but apex predators are difficult to tame and remain dangerous no matter how well trained.
Obstacle 2: Cost
Dragons would be ridiculously expensive to keep. Large carnivorous animals are expensive in any pre-industrial society; meat was very costly. Tame dragons would need huge amounts of meat per individual, and even more for:

Restraints big and strong enough to contain them

A large lair / stable for off-duty time

Saddles, reins, and other equipment scaled to a dragon

A treasure hoard, if the species needs one
Obstacle 3: Anatomy
Horses are our main mounts because their anatomy is well-suited to being ridden. Dragons are not so neat.

A dragon's back may not be smooth

Wings, scales, and back-spines complicate saddle design

A dragon has to be a specific size — too small can't bear a rider, too large and a human can't mount or control them
Obstacle 4: Behavior
A dragon needs to be tameable AND trainable AND have the right attitude.

Large, solitary apex predators tend to be territorial and defiant

They may not get along with other dragons, or with their supposed master

Hibernation or seasonal migration removes them from service for long periods

Fire-breathing used for territorial marking risks burning your home down in a rampage
Obstacle 5: Altitude
Lack of oxygen and high flying speed. Rarely mentioned — flying at high altitudes means lack of oxygen and cold, strong winds. Riders need:

Heavy jackets and goggles for warmth and wind

Some kind of oxygen tank or enchanted mask to allow breathing

Safety harnesses and straps so a strong gust doesn't throw them off
Sandbox
Use the Cast Sandbox above to test specific "what if a Dragon-kin tried to do X with their bonded Aero" scenarios against these rules.

articles.title: Scene — The Cold Camp
articles.summary: Artemis vs Atla after she learns he is Jiyra. He shifts to keep her warm.

content.text: Artemis has just learned what Atla is — a bloodthirsty, monstrous Jiyra. The walk goes cold and silent. She walks ahead. He falls behind. She relents and they set camp.

I shiver, clutching my arms in an attempt to remain warm. My attempt fails, and it's almost laughable because Atla could easily shift into a dragon and keep us both warm, but he refuses. Because of what? He's too scared of himself? I huff and remain cold.
"We should set up camp, Artemis…" Atla says, from behind me. I decide to ignore him and keep walking. He deserves this treatment.
After a minute, I finally notice his light tread has silenced. I turn — he stands a good thirty feet away. I let out a long, exasperated sigh before returning to his side.
"Cold?" I ask, examining his stormy gray eyes. He stares at his worn-down boots. "That's what I thought. Fine, let's set up camp. Like you said."
"Alright," he says, still avoiding eye contact.
After they pitch camp, Artemis slips into her sleeping bag. Atla refuses to do the same — he'll "keep a lookout" in lifeless woods.

I startle awake during the moon's highest solstice, my lips purple with cold. Shivering uncontrollably, hands and feet numb. I stumble toward Atla's silhouette. He turns, sees my frost-bitten state, and approaches with care, grabbing my arms as I nearly fall.
I press myself to him, seeking warmth. He wraps me up, leads me back to the tent, and begins to leave.

His skin starts changing to a molten blue color, cracking and shifting. His flesh stretches, his limbs elongate, and he grows to a colossal size. Then, as suddenly as it started, it ends. A sixty-foot dragon stands outside of my tent, where Atla stood. His massive head pushes the tent aside and his familiar stormy-gray eyes peer back at me. I stumble forward, landing on his nose. He lifts me into the air and nestles my body right next to his great thumping heart, massive leathery wings enclosing me like a tent.
The warmth he holds inside himself floods into my skin, melting the frost that had been collecting on my lashes.
Atla's dragon form: molten blue, 60 feet, leathery wings, stormy-gray eyes
Trigger for shift: protecting Artemis from death by cold
After this point, Artemis can't pretend he's only a monster

articles.contentText:
Setup
Artemis has just learned what Atla is — a bloodthirsty, monstrous Jiyra. The walk goes cold and silent. She walks ahead. He falls behind. She relents and they set camp.
The Scene
I shiver, clutching my arms in an attempt to remain warm. My attempt fails, and it's almost laughable because Atla could easily shift into a dragon and keep us both warm, but he refuses. Because of what? He's too scared of himself? I huff and remain cold.
"We should set up camp, Artemis…" Atla says, from behind me. I decide to ignore him and keep walking. He deserves this treatment.
After a minute, I finally notice his light tread has silenced. I turn — he stands a good thirty feet away. I let out a long, exasperated sigh before returning to his side.
"Cold?" I ask, examining his stormy gray eyes. He stares at his worn-down boots. "That's what I thought. Fine, let's set up camp. Like you said."
"Alright," he says, still avoiding eye contact.
After they pitch camp, Artemis slips into her sleeping bag. Atla refuses to do the same — he'll "keep a lookout" in lifeless woods.
The Shift
I startle awake during the moon's highest solstice, my lips purple with cold. Shivering uncontrollably, hands and feet numb. I stumble toward Atla's silhouette. He turns, sees my frost-bitten state, and approaches with care, grabbing my arms as I nearly fall.
"Arty, are you alright?"
I press myself to him, seeking warmth. He wraps me up, leads me back to the tent, and begins to leave.
"Atla, wait—"
"Don't worry, I'll be back…"
His skin starts changing to a molten blue color, cracking and shifting. His flesh stretches, his limbs elongate, and he grows to a colossal size. Then, as suddenly as it started, it ends. A sixty-foot dragon stands outside of my tent, where Atla stood. His massive head pushes the tent aside and his familiar stormy-gray eyes peer back at me. I stumble forward, landing on his nose. He lifts me into the air and nestles my body right next to his great thumping heart, massive leathery wings enclosing me like a tent.
The warmth he holds inside himself floods into my skin, melting the frost that had been collecting on my lashes.
Notes

Atla's dragon form: molten blue, 60 feet, leathery wings, stormy-gray eyes

Trigger for shift: protecting Artemis from death by cold

After this point, Artemis can't pretend he's only a monster

articles.title: Scene — Flying to Siora
articles.summary: A wyvern POV — diving toward Siora reading on her crooked bench.

content.text: First-person wyvern POV. Possibly a Maned Aero given the closeness — or a Dragon-kin shifted into a wyvern? Decide later.

I soar through the air, wings finding rhythm, beating against the cool, gentle breeze. As my gaze sweeps the human village, it sets on one specific location — a bench, slightly crooked, sitting next to a towering building. There, on that bench, is Siora. She's reading again, and it almost makes me laugh. She's always there. At least I know where I can find her.

My wings cut through the air as I hurtle myself downwards, at a swift speed, aiming directly for her. She hardly has time to look up before I drop onto her, tangling us both up in my huge, leathery wings. She is startled for a moment, before registering it is me and throwing her arms around me, despite her typical lack of physical contact.

When I leap backwards, we're both grinning, all of my canines and sharp, pearly fangs gleaming from behind my reptilian lips. I leap around, feeling almost like a cat, hyper with the zoomies. I flap my wings, leaping back and forth, before doing three speedy loops around the tower, in the air. When I land again, Siora is smiling up at me, a small laugh emanating from her.
Siora is a recurring sub-character (human?). She reads on the same bench daily.
POV creature is comfortable enough with her to make physical contact she normally avoids.
Tone: playful, joyful — a beat of brightness in a tense world.

POV
First-person wyvern POV. Possibly a Maned Aero given the closeness — or a Dragon-kin shifted into a wyvern? Decide later.
The Scene
I soar through the air, wings finding rhythm, beating against the cool, gentle breeze. As my gaze sweeps the human village, it sets on one specific location — a bench, slightly crooked, sitting next to a towering building. There, on that bench, is Siora. She's reading again, and it almost makes me laugh. She's always there. At least I know where I can find her.
My wings cut through the air as I hurtle myself downwards, at a swift speed, aiming directly for her. She hardly has time to look up before I drop onto her, tangling us both up in my huge, leathery wings. She is startled for a moment, before registering it is me and throwing her arms around me, despite her typical lack of physical contact.
When I leap backwards, we're both grinning, all of my canines and sharp, pearly fangs gleaming from behind my reptilian lips. I leap around, feeling almost like a cat, hyper with the zoomies. I flap my wings, leaping back and forth, before doing three speedy loops around the tower, in the air. When I land again, Siora is smiling up at me, a small laugh emanating from her.
Notes

Siora is a recurring sub-character (human?). She reads on the same bench daily.

POV creature is comfortable enough with her to make physical contact she normally avoids.

Tone: playful, joyful — a beat of brightness in a tense world.

articles.title: Ancient vs. Modern Draconia
articles.summary: The split between the small, surviving species and the vast, extinct ancestors.

content.text: From the Ancient Days through the Modern Day.
Modern Draconia are the Draconians that live in the present age. They are smaller, more common versions of their ancestors. Modern Draconia consists of:
Ancient Draconia were the Draconians of the Ancient Days. All are now extinct:
What caused the Ancient Draconia to die out?
Did the Jiyra play a role? (The creation myth implies a moment where "Jiyra came upon the Dragon".)
Were Drakes, Wyrms, Sea Serpents lost in the same event, or different ones?
Could any Ancient Draconia still survive, hidden?

articles.contentText:
Era
From the Ancient Days through the Modern Day.
Modern Draconia
Modern Draconia are the Draconians that live in the present age. They are smaller, more common versions of their ancestors. Modern Draconia consists of:

Wyvernia (Wyverns, Aero, all subspecies)

Dragon-kin

Jiyra
Ancient Draconia
Ancient Draconia were the Draconians of the Ancient Days. All are now extinct:

Dragons

Drakes

Wyrms

Sea Serpents
Open Questions

What caused the Ancient Draconia to die out?

Did the Jiyra play a role? (The creation myth implies a moment where "Jiyra came upon the Dragon".)

Were Drakes, Wyrms, Sea Serpents lost in the same event, or different ones?

Could any Ancient Draconia still survive, hidden?
